meta.setting: The road into French Gulch from Clear Creek, Shasta, and the high timber
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with road friction, freight, witnesses, mud, law heat, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush movement, mining communities, roads, and regional settlement pressure.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, road-town texture, and old county movement.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the French Gulch Road region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Ruts, Grades, and Return Smoke
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: object, weather or track, plain meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing the road as travel problem, social pressure, resource, and scene engine
closing blockquote that gives a generated trail sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate the road as the first playable artery: mud, grade, freight, witnesses, ambush bends, and town gossip moving both ways.
Make it a travel engine rather than scenery; every paragraph should imply a cost, return route, or person watching.
Use ruts, smoke, hoof churn, broken spokes, bridge delays, and porch rumor as the road's language.
Keep French Gulch close but not safe; the road should feel like it can change the town before riders arrive.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The road should feel like it has chewed every wheel that trusted it. It is not a tutorial path; it is a debt collector made of mud.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A French Gulch Road scene should ask what the riders bring in, what follows them, and who sees them before the town admits it.
must_preserve: The generated page must include image path images/french-gulch-road.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the road scenic only; it must create choices and costs
do not use modern pavement, cars, electric signs, modern slang, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about road pressure, travel cost, witnesses, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
